retry_reason: refcount_dec_and_test always succeeds; a double-put would be a use-after-free bug, so retry is pointless.
contract_summary: This is a put wrapper that releases one reference on a master VLAN object (`masterv`). It unconditionally decrements the refcount if `masterv` is a master VLAN; otherwise it returns early. When the refcount reaches zero, the function performs full cleanup including rhtable removal, list deletion, multicast teardown, and schedules RCU freeing. After calling this function, the caller must not use `masterv` again (it may be freed). There is no return value to check, but the caller must ensure the object is alive before the call to avoid a double-put / use-after-free.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to decrement refcount and free the master VLAN if the last reference; only ancillary cleanup occurs on the final put.

check.reason: refcount_dec_and_test is called on &masterv->refcnt, which decrements the refcount. The access path masterv.refcnt.refs correctly traces from parameter masterv through the refcnt field (type refcount_t) to the refs (atomic_t) field. The segment count matches (3 segments, 3 types). The direction 'put' is consistent.
